name: Pico Montero and Los Alcornocales Park Loop
name_original: Pico del Montero - Parque Natural los Alcornocales – circular desde Alcalá de los Gazules
description: This difficult 68.2-mile road cycling route through Los Alcornocales Natural Park offers stunning views and a challenging climb to Pico del
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules are set within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features extensive cork oak forests, lush valleys, and dramatic rocky peaks, providing varied landscapes. The town itself is situated on a hill, contributing to the area's varied topography and offering elevated views. This blend of natural beauty and challenging ascents makes it a compelling destination for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules The most popular road cycling route is Pico Montero and Los Alcornocales Park Loop, a 68.2 miles (110.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes and stunning vistas within the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint – Alcalá de los Gazules loop from Alcalá de los Gazules, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.8 km) path. This route includes a substantial ascent, leading to panoramic views from the viewpoint. Local road cyclists also love the Alcalá de los Gazules – Benalup - Casas Viejas loop from Alcalá de los Gazules, a 55.7 miles (89.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Alcalá De Los Gazules is defined by cork oak forests, lush valleys, and rocky hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Alcalá De Los Gazules are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 180 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Los Gazules's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: The road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules are characterized by diverse terrain within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park. You'll encounter extensive cork oak forests, lush valleys, and dramatic rocky peaks. The town itself is situated on a hill, contributing to varied topography with both challenging ascents and scenic descents. Routes can range from moderate to difficult, often featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there many challenging road cycling routes in the Alcalá De Los Gazules area?
A: Yes, Alcalá De Los Gazules offers several challenging road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961432240/pico-montero-and-los-alcornocales-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Montero and Los Alcornocales Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 68.2-mile (110.0 km) route with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19300363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de los Gazules – Benalup - Casas Viejas loop from Alcalá de los Gazules&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 55.7 miles (89.6 km) through varied terrain. In total, 5 out of 16 routes in the area are rated as difficult.

Q: What are the best times of year for road cycling in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: While road cycling is enjoyable for much of the year, September and December are particularly popular months for cyclists in Alcalá De Los Gazules. The Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant conditions outside of the hottest summer months, making spring and autumn ideal for exploring the region's routes.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling around Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: Road cycling routes in this region are primarily set within the stunning Los Alcornocales Natural Park, one of Europe's largest cork oak forests. You'll experience dense forests, meandering rivers, lush valleys, and dramatic rocky peaks. Elevated points, such as those found on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31514688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Mediterranean vegetation and the surrounding natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available from Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules are designed as loops, starting and ending in the town. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961432240/pico-montero-and-los-alcornocales-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Montero and Los Alcornocales Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31514688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint – Alcalá de los Gazules loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de los Gazules – Barbate Reservoir Dam Path loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for round trips.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: The road cycling experience in Alcalá De Los Gazules is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging ascents, and the stunning vistas offered within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park. The blend of natural beauty and varied routes makes it a compelling destination for many.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or near interesting landmarks and natural attractions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31513732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de los Gazules – Barbate Reservoir Dam Path loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbate Reservoir Dam Path&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions you might encounter include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Redondo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5644866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Trail in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: The road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules tend to be more geared towards moderate to difficult skill levels, with 11 moderate and 5 difficult routes listed. There are currently no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' for road cycling, suggesting that most routes involve some level of challenge, including ascents and varied terrain. Therefore, truly family-friendly options for road cycling might be limited.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: Alcalá De Los Gazules, as a charming white village, offers various parking options within and around the town. While specific dedicated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, general public parking should be available, especially near the town center or at the start points of popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient spots.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcalá De Los Gazules?
A: There are 16 road cycling routes available around Alcalá De Los Gazules, offering a good variety for cyclists. These routes are rated by difficulty, with 11 moderate and 5 difficult options to choose from.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: While the routes themselves often traverse natural parkland, Alcalá De Los Gazules and nearby villages provide charming spots for refreshments. The town itself has steep, whitewashed streets with local cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. Planning your route to pass through these towns will allow for convenient stops.

Q: What is the average duration for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Alcalá De Los Gazules varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31515250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benalup - Casas Viejas – Alcalá de los Gazules loop&lt;/a&gt; can take around 1 hour 55 minutes, while a difficult route such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961432240/pico-montero-and-los-alcornocales-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Montero and Los Alcornocales Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take approximately 5 hours 41 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. It involves a substantial effort, with over 1,850 meters (6,100 feet) of elevation gain over approximately 110 km. Be prepared for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Peak of Montero cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, the terrain includes a mix of paved and potentially unpaved sections. Most of the route, about 98.9 km, is asphalt, but some segments, around 10.9 km, might be rougher or unpaved, possibly requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this challenging route is approximately 5 hours and 41 minutes of active cycling time. However, given its difficulty and length, it can easily take over 5 hours, not including stops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning scenery within Los Alcornocales Natural Park. Key highlights include the Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint, the challenging ascent to Pico del Montero, and the El Picacho Recreation Area. You'll also pass through the historic town of Alcalá de los Gazules, which serves as the starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Alcalá de los Gazules?
faqs.answer: Alcalá de los Gazules is the starting point for this circular route. As a historic town, you should look for public parking areas within or on the outskirts of the town. Specific parking details are not provided, but general street parking or designated town parking lots would be your best options.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is not suitable for beginners or families. It is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its length (110 km) and significant elevation gain (over 1,850 meters). There are also potentially unpaved sections that add to the challenge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Los Alcornocales Natural Park experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the best times to cycle, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be wet and cool, making cycling more challenging.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Los Alcornocales Natural Park generally does not require an entrance fee for public access. However, it's important to note that accessing the specific area of Pico del Montero might require special permission, as it is within a national park. Always check local regulations before your trip, especially for sensitive areas.

faqs.question: Is the Peak of Montero cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for race biking, and due to its length, difficulty, and the nature of road cycling, it is generally not considered suitable for dogs. While Los Alcornocales Natural Park may have dog-friendly hiking trails, this specific cycling route is not recommended for pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails within the park. Notably, it covers significant sections of Picacho Peguera (24% of the route), [CIMA CD10] Pico Montero (EVA-11) (13%), and [CIMA CD03] Gáliz * Alcalá de los Gazules (12%). Smaller portions also overlap with Sendero Subida Al Aljibe, Sendero Subida Al Picacho, Sendero Travesía del Aljibe, and Sendero Garganta de Puerto Oscuro.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Los Alcornocales Natural Park is a Special Bird Protection Zone (ZEPA) and a Place of Community Interest (LIC), making it rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, and the park is known for its ancient cork oak forests. Keep an eye out for local fauna, but remember to observe from a distance and respect their natural habitat.
